# Generated by openai (gpt-4o)
# Prompt: Create Linkerd default authorization policy for a namespace. Configure policy to handle services without explicit authorization rules. Set up default behavior for service-to-service communication. This is for our service mesh policy framework.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent linkerd policy default allow.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: linkerd_policy_default_allow

apiVersion: policy.linkerd.io/v1beta1
kind: AuthorizationPolicy
metadata:
  name: default-deny
  namespace: your-namespace
spec:
  targetRef:
    kind: Namespace
    name: your-namespace
  rules:
  - from:
    - kind: ServiceAccount
      name: trusted-service-account
  - to:
    - kind: Service
      name: trusted-service
  default:
    action: DENY
